perturbation.original_text: Securities Law Compliance You acknowledge that the Evaluation Materials represent, in part, material, non-public information regarding the Company and its prospects. Accordingly, you acknowledge that you and your representatives understand that the securities laws of the United States prohibit any person who has material, non-public information concerning the Company or a Possible Transaction from purchasing or selling securities in reliance upon such information or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Other This agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof Securities Law Compliance You acknowledge that the Evaluation Materials represent, in part, material, non-public information regarding the Company and its prospects. Accordingly, you acknowledge that you and your representatives understand that the securities laws of the United States prohibit any person who has material, non-public information concerning the Company or a Possible Transaction from purchasing or selling securities in reliance upon such information or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.explanation: The placement of the securities law compliance statement under 'Other' terms diminishes its prominence and clarity. This could lead to a lack of awareness regarding insider trading prohibitions.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the 'Securities Law Compliance' section to 'Other' obscures the critical information about insider trading prohibitions, which violates the intent of Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The law requires clear and conspicuous disclosure of such information to prevent misuse of material non-public information.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The structural flaw, as described, directly impacts the clarity and prominence of the securities law compliance statement. Moving it under 'Other' diminishes its significance, potentially leading to a lack of awareness and increasing the risk of insider trading. This contradicts the purpose of securities laws, specifically Rule 10b-5, which aims to prevent manipulative and deceptive devices in connection with the purchase or sale of securities, as stated in Scraped Law Snippet 1: 'It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce... (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud... (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security.' By obscuring the compliance statement, the modified text creates an environment where such fraudulent activities are more likely to occur, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Standstill You agree that until two years from the date of this agreement, without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company, neither you nor any of your representatives will (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger. Except as provided above, you also agree during such period not to request the Company (or its directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives) to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph.
perturbation.changed_text: No Representation or Warranty Although the Company and Mesirow have endeavored to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that none of the Company, Mesirow or any of the Company’s other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and none of the Company, Mesirow or any of the Company’s other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to you or any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any of your representatives. Standstill You agree that until two years from the date of this agreement, without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company, neither you nor any of your representatives will (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger. Except as provided above, you also agree during such period not to request the Company (or its directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives) to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the standstill clause to the 'No Representation or Warranty' section weakens its enforceability. The Standstill agreement restricting the buyer from acquiring securities, assets, etc. is lost and not easy to find when placed under the company not making any representation or warranty section.
perturbation.law_explanation: The relocation of the standstill provision impedes its ability to prevent actions regulated under Section 13(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, such as the formation of a group to influence the company's management. The prominence of the standstill agreement is lessened when hidden under 'No Representation or Warranty', weakening its preventive effect and making it less apparent, making it a contradiction with the law.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78 l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security— (1) such information and documents (and such copies thereof) as the Commission shall require to keep reasonably current the information and documents required to be included in or filed with an application or registration statement filed pursuant to section 78l of this title , except that the Commission may not require the filing of any material contract wholly executed before July 1, 1962 . (2) such annual reports (and such copies thereof), certified if required by the rules and regulations of the Commission by independent public accountants, and such quarterly reports (and such copies thereof), as the Commission may prescribe. Every issuer of a security registered on a national securities exchange shall also file a duplicate original of such information, documents, and reports with the exchange . In any registration statement, periodic report, or other reports to be filed with the Commission , an emerging growth company need not present selected financial data in accordance with section 229.301 of title 17, Code of Federal Regulations, for any period prior to the earliest audited period presented in connection with its first registration statement that became effective under this chapter or the Securities Act of 1933 [ 15 U.S.C. 77a et seq.] and, with respect to any such statement or reports, an emerging growth company may not be required to
perturbation.justification: The original contract clearly outlines a standstill agreement. The changed text buries this clause under a disclaimer about the accuracy of evaluation materials. This structural flaw, as described in the Perturbation Explanation and the Law Explanation, undermines the enforceability and prominence of the standstill agreement, hindering its intended function under securities regulations. While the snippets do not directly state this, the law explanation strongly suggests the relocation impedes the agreement's ability to prevent actions regulated under Section 13(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, thus the contract change creates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Return of Documents Upon the Company’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to Mesirow or employees or representatives of the Company specified by Mesirow. Return of Documents Upon the Company’s request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Return of Documents' clause under 'No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation' reduces the likelihood that the recipient will fully understand their obligation to return or destroy confidential information, as they may not look for it in that section.
perturbation.law_explanation: By moving the document return clause under 'No Unauthorized Contact', the contract doesn't prominently display the obligation for document return, which is key to preventing the misappropriation of trade secrets, a violation of UTSA § 2. The responsibility to return documents is diminished when buried in a less relevant section.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation and law explanation both state that the relocation of the 'Return of Documents' clause under 'No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation' diminishes the prominence and discoverability of this critical obligation. This structural flaw, according to the definition provided for Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction, creates a situation where a required legal disclosure (the document return clause) is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. UTSA § 2 aims to prevent misappropriation of trade secrets, and obscuring the document return obligation weakens this protection. The changed text impairs the enforceability of the document return requirement by burying it within an unrelated section. Therefore, the modification introduces a significant legal contradiction.
